data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $6.8427 million yesterday, marking the first net inflow after five days of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $6.8427 million yesterday (January 26th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) saw the largest single-day net inflow of $15.9258 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $62.919 billion. This was followed by the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF (BTC), with a net inflow of $7.7454 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.959 billion. Conversely, the Bitwise ETF (BITB) saw the largest single-day net outflow of $10.9668 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $2.16 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $113.539 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.48%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $56.501 billion.

data.title: South Korean Authorities Uncover Major Counterfeit Luxury Goods Operation
data.description: South Korean customs officials have dismantled a large-scale counterfeit luxury goods operation, according to Foresight News. The Incheon Main Customs Office seized approximately 77,000 fake items, which, if genuine, would be valued at around 120 billion Korean won. The suspects reportedly profited 16.5 billion won by selling these counterfeit goods through online platforms. A portion of the illicit earnings, approximately 500 million won, was converted into cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and stored in hardware wallets to evade seizure. Authorities have confiscated these digital assets and frozen additional assets, including apartments, hotels, and luxury vehicles, worth about 8 billion won.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Discusses User State and Data Structure Differences
data.description: Vitalik Buterin responded to a user's perspective on the nature of state and data structure, highlighting the differences in editing capabilities. According to Foresight News, Buterin explained that the variations lie in the ability to edit: simple structures only support append operations, medium structures allow for linear list modifications, and complex structures involve key-value storage where users can create new values at any location.

data.title: Potential Announcement of New Federal Reserve Chair This Week
data.description:
Qinbafrank posted on X. The nomination for the new Federal Reserve Chair might be announced this week. Speculation arises whether U.S. President Donald Trump will reveal the new chair candidate during Jerome Powell's press conference following the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision early Thursday morning. If this occurs, market attention may shift from Powell's statements to analyzing the policy positions of the new chair.

This week's Federal Reserve meeting is expected to likely pause interest rate cuts. The key focus will be whether the pause is hawkish or dovish. It is anticipated that Powell might lean towards a neutral to hawkish stance. The main point of interest now is whether Trump will announce the new Federal Reserve Chair early Thursday morning.

data.title: U.S. Spot Ethereum ETFs Experience Significant Inflows
data.description: U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs recorded a net inflow of $110 million on January 26, marking a reversal after four consecutive days of outflows. According to NS3.AI, this positive shift was primarily driven by Fidelity's FETH, which saw an influx of $137 million. In contrast, BlackRock's ETHA faced outflows amounting to $20.16 million.

data.title: European Commission Investigates X Over Digital Services Act Compliance
data.description: The European Commission has launched an investigation into X's compliance with the Digital Services Act, focusing on whether its Grok AI chatbot has generated illegal content, including sexually explicit images of children. According to NS3.AI, despite X's recent measures to restrict features to paid subscribers and geoblock certain regions, a substantial amount of illicit images continues to be accessible. This investigation builds on previous EU penalties imposed on X for deceptive practices and intensifies scrutiny on Grok's involvement in disseminating prohibited content.

data.title: Matrixport: Central banks' continued gold purchases may explain the strength of gold and the relative pressure on Bitcoin.
data.description: Matrixport's chart analysis points out that the continued rise in gold prices is primarily driven by renewed concerns about the weakening purchasing power of the US dollar. Against the backdrop of Trump's renewed push for tariffs on Europe, the dollar is under pressure, while discussions about foreign central banks potentially reducing their holdings of US Treasury bonds and shifting more foreign exchange reserves to gold have increased significantly. The analysis suggests that gold's relative strength is closely related to official sector demand. Central banks worldwide are continuously increasing their gold holdings, with the People's Bank of China (PBoC) being particularly closely watched, providing sustained buying support for gold prices. In contrast, Bitcoin is still less commonly included in central bank reserve diversification frameworks in terms of public disclosure. For policymakers, gold remains a more mainstream asset and more in line with existing reserve management systems, while Bitcoin has not yet been widely accepted in official foreign exchange reserves. Matrixport suggests that this divergence in central bank asset allocation may, to some extent, explain the recent strength of gold and the relative weakness of Bitcoin.

data.title: Tokenization's Role in Global Finance Infrastructure Expands
data.description: Tokenization is transitioning from a speculative idea to a crucial element in global financial infrastructure, enhancing settlement speed, transparency, and operational efficiency. According to NS3.AI, while mainstream Western markets face perception challenges, emerging economies are utilizing tokenization to address legacy inefficiencies. Regulatory strategies differ worldwide, with Europe emphasizing formalization and clarity through the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework, while Gulf nations are implementing phased, supervised rollouts.

data.title: Solana Spot ETF Sees Significant Inflow on January 26
data.description:
On January 27, Solana spot ETF experienced a net inflow of $2.46 million on January 26, according to PANews. The Bitwise SOL ETF BSOL was the sole contributor to this inflow, marking a single-day net inflow of $2.46 million and bringing its historical total net inflow to $684 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETF stands at $1.05 billion, with a Solana net asset ratio of 1.50%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $876 million.

data.title: U.S. Data Center Construction Surges Amid AI Infrastructure Demand
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. The construction of data centers in the United States has significantly increased over the past three years, reflecting a shift in capital expenditure towards AI infrastructure rather than traditional office spaces. This trend indicates a change in economic expansion drivers, with data centers now serving as the backbone of growth instead of office buildings. The prosperity of regions is increasingly determined by factors such as electricity, grid connectivity, cooling systems, and fiber optics, which dictate the capacity for computational power.

The expansion of data centers highlights a fundamental shift where electricity is treated as a raw material and computational power as the product. The rise in data center construction is a visible outcome, but the underlying factor is the growing competition for grid resources. Looking ahead to 2026, there is optimism about the prospects for U.S. electricity stocks.

data.title: Bitcoin-Related Job Opportunities Expected to Rise by 2025
data.description:
Bitcoin-related job opportunities are projected to increase by 6% in 2025, reaching a total of 1,801 positions, according to PANews. The report, released by Bitvocation, highlights a significant rise in non-technical roles, which will account for 74% of all job openings, up from 69% in 2024. The most sought-after non-technical positions include product managers, executive assistants, marketing managers, directors, and product designers. Notably, director roles are expected to grow tenfold, indicating an expansion in operations among Bitcoin companies.

The report also reveals that over 150 companies focused solely on Bitcoin are actively hiring, with Riot Platforms and Lightspark leading the recruitment efforts. The top ten companies have seen a 122% increase in hiring compared to the previous year. The United States remains the leader with approximately 500 positions, while Singapore has experienced a remarkable 158% year-on-year growth, securing the second spot.

Remote Bitcoin positions have decreased from 53% to 45%, reflecting a shift in hiring trends within the industry.

data.title: DXY Decline Could Boost Cryptocurrency Market
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X. The Dollar Index (DXY) has recently lost its support level, leading to a decline. This development is seen as a potential tailwind for the cryptocurrency market, particularly benefiting Ethereum (ETH) and alternative cryptocurrencies (alts). Historically, a weak DXY often precedes positive movements in the crypto market before prices react.
